title: Verify the 1.1.0 build on a clean machine — local engine was never tested on ComfyUI 0.27
description: USER TESTS THIS (install the shipped 1.1.0 build, launch, generate locally). If it fails, open an agent session from this card. ROOT: the engine version lives in TWO files that drifted (see memory project_release_engine_version_desync). dev_configs/node_lock.json `core.tag` moved v0.26.0 -> v0.27.0 in commit 6884514b (MPI-148, 2026-07-03). dev_configs/system_dependencies.json `engine.version` moved 0.26.0 -> 0.27.0 only in 41c59093 — the 1.1.0 release commit ITSELF (2026-07-09). VERIFIED with `git merge-base --is-ancestor 6884514b 41c59093` => node_lock was ALREADY 0.27 when 1.1.0 was cut. CONSEQUENCE: the LOCAL engine download gate is `installedVersion !== requiredVersion` where requiredVersion = COMFY_VERSION read from system_dependencies.json (routes/engine.js:509-540 /engine/version-check). During ALL 1.1.0 testing that file said 0.26.0 and the dev machine's engine/.mpi_engine_version said 0.26.0 => gate compared EQUAL => no upgrade => every local-engine test ran on 0.26 while node_lock pinned 0.27-era node commits. The REMOTE/RunPod path reads node_lock (NOT system_dependencies), so the Pod ran 0.27 all along. 1.1.0 IS the RunPod release, so its own engine was tested on what it ships; what is UNTESTED is the LOCAL engine on 0.27. NOT a 'nobody ran it' situation — a real but bounded gap. INCIDENTAL (2026-07-10, agent launched the app on the bumped branch): the 0.26->0.27 local upgrade fired for the first time, pulled ~2GB (ComfyUI_windows_portable_nvidia.7z v0.27.0) + re-pinned 11 UW custom nodes, and the parallel node installer RACED: `python install.py` ran against comfyui-frame-interpolation before its extraction finished ('can't open file ... install.py', exit 2), aborting the batch and leaving ComfyUI-Impact-Subpack + RES4LYF undownloaded. /engine/deps-status reported {missingDeps:[Impact-Subpack,RES4LYF], driftedDeps:[Frame-Interpolation]}. USER PRESSED RETRY AND IT RECOVERED (models 7/11 -> 9/11, app booted clean). That race is a SEPARATE latent bug worth its own card if it recurs — it is not a 0.27 incompatibility. WHAT TO TEST: install the shipped 1.1.0 artifact on a clean machine (no pre-existing engine/), let it download 0.27, confirm the 11 UW nodes install WITHOUT a retry, then generate on the LOCAL engine with at least one model per family (SDXL, Chroma, LTX, Wan) and confirm the pinned node commits work against core 0.27. Per feedback_runpod_not_local_engine_proof: a RunPod run does NOT verify this — it must be the local engine. Per feedback_check_both_engine_paths: if a fix is needed, check whether the remote twin needs it too.
